title: Butter on Cotton
stainChemistry: Butter is ~80% butterfat + small milk protein + salt + water. The protein fraction is small enough that protein-before-heat ordering isn't as critical as for milk / chocolate, but not zero — if a butter stain is hot-washed immediately without surfactant, the milk protein fraction can yellow slightly. Cotton tolerates NSD, Dawn, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash. Butter is one of the simpler kitchen grease stains because saturated fats respond well to Dawn.
whyThisWorks: Scrape bulk before chemistry. Dawn emulsifies butterfat quickly. Hot wash finishes. For a small protein-yellow shadow (rare on butter), a dilute peroxide pass clears it.
defaultAssumption: Butter on cotton is a trivially easy stain in most cases. Kid / breakfast context. Aged or hot-dried butter is a bit harder but still in DIY range.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dull blade, cold water, white cotton towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid butter off with a dull blade. Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric — loosens remaining fat, removes the small milk protein fraction before heat.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn dish soap + warm water, soft brush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Work Dawn into the spot with a soft toothbrush for 1 min. Dwell 5 min. Rinse warm.
Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

steps: 1. Scrape bulk. Cold flush from the back.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold flush; warm Dawn; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Small milk protein fraction — cold flush before hot wash
Fresh butter is trivially easy; don't over-treat

homeSolutions.instruction: Use a dull butter knife or spoon edge to scrape any solid butter off the fabric. Don't rub.
Hold the BACK of the fabric under cold running water for 30–60 seconds. The small milk-protein fraction in butter sets if you skip this and go straight to warm water.
Work a drop of Dawn into the spot with a fingertip or soft toothbrush. Dawn is chemistry-designed for butterfat. Rinse warm. Repeat if an oily feel remains.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — residual butterfat polymerizes under heat like any fat.

steps: 2. Cold water flush from the BACK.
4. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Rarely needs escalation. Only if butter was hot-dried and polymerized.
warnings: Inspect BEFORE the dryer — heat polymerizes fat

neverDo: Never skip the cold flush — milk protein fraction sets
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, Dawn, hot wash

escalation.when: Rare — only if butter was hot-dried and polymerized.
escalation.whatToTell: "Butter on cotton, age [fresh / hot-dried], home-treated with Dawn + hot wash."

contextQuestions: Plain butter, garlic butter, or compound (herbs / oils)?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Plain butter is one of the easiest common stains. Trivially Dawn-emulsifiable. Compound butters add their own ingredients to treat.
